maintenanceScenario: A maintainer removes a compatibility export because the graph-source projection already contains the same visible fields.
vibeCodingRisk: Without the recovered compatibility intent, an AI-assisted cleanup may delete fallback evidence needed for rollback, audit comparison, or external consumers.

intentRecords.statement: Retain the compatibility export until rollback/fallback and external audit consumers have an explicit retirement decision.
intentRecords.maintenanceRiskIfMissing: The export may be deleted as duplicate, breaking rollback review or audit traceability even though current generated views still pass.
intentRecords.preservationRule: Do not retire compatibility exports during graph-source cleanup without an explicit retirement decision and replacement evidence.
intentRecords.nonGoal: Do not interpret graph-source-backed generation as permission to delete tree-native or compatibility artifacts.
intentRecords.fallbackReason: The compatibility export remains the fallback/reference artifact when generated graph-source projections are stale, blocked, or disputed.
intentRecords.evidenceReason: Maintenance review should show both the graph-source projection and the retained compatibility export when evaluating retirement readiness.
intentRecords.compatibilityReason: Retrofit PBE must preserve legacy/audit vocabulary long enough for users to compare old and new maintenance surfaces.

readModelIntentFields.projectionBoundary: Read-model projections may expose recovered intent as maintenance Evidence, but cannot silently approve artifact retirement.

boundaries.sourceAuthorityBoundary: This fixture demonstrates retrofit intent recovery only; it does not retire compatibility artifacts or expand source authority.
boundaries.nonPromotionStatement: This fixture is not Graph-source promotion, enforcement, required-check configuration, or tree retirement.
boundaries.userAcceptanceBoundary: Only the user can approve retiring the compatibility/fallback artifact.
